reference.title: Workflow step patterns reference
reference.shortdesc: A workflow's steps field is a comma-separated string of source-target tokens. Twelve patterns are recognised. Combine them to express any conditional rule dependency.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Complete reference for all twelve markdown-validator workflow step patterns with examples of each pattern in context.

row.entry: Start: load rule N as the initial state.
Rule N result becomes the decision point (the branch condition).
Merge state becomes the decision point.
Used in loops or multi-rule merges
If the current decision is True (pass), load rule N.
If the current decision is False (fail), load rule N.
If the current decision is True, reverse (negate) it.
Rarely used; present to enable inversion patterns
If the current decision is False, reverse (negate) it.
Rule N result exits into the merge state.
Merge state exits to rule N.
Rule N result ends the workflow.
Both rules must pass (logical AND). The second N is the rule that follows the first.

section.title: Recipe: Single rule check
section.p: Start with rule 1. Rule 1's result ends the workflow.

section.title: Recipe: Conditional check (run rule B only when rule A passes)
section.p: Rule A is the decision. If True, rule B runs and its result feeds into merge. If False, the sentinel rule (a known-passing rule) feeds into merge. Merge ends the workflow.

section.title: Recipe: Both rules must pass (AND)
section.p: Start with rule 1. Rule 1 must pass before rule 2 is checked. Rule 2's result ends the workflow.

ul.li: Both S-1,1-E and (S,1)(1,E) formats are accepted; the parenthesis format is normalised at load time.
Rule IDs referenced in step tokens must exist in the rules section of the same rule set file.
